proposed_hypotheses.text: Each baseline feature in the dataset has at least some univariate association with pfs_months; binary features with strong directional effects are candidates for being treatments, biomarkers, or stage indicators.

analyses.code: for col in features: t-test or ANOVA or pearsonr against pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: Strong univariate associations: feature_015 (Pearson r=+0.757, p<1e-300, range 30-90), feature_023 (binary, mean diff -1.44 months, p<1e-300), feature_014 (3-level ordinal: 0->4.33, 1->3.19, 2->2.15 months; ANOVA p<1e-300), feature_011 (binary, -0.92 months, p<1e-300), feature_006 (squamous mean 2.81 vs adeno 3.67, p<1e-300), feature_001 (smoking: never 3.67, former 3.61, current 2.99, p=4e-187), feature_017 (continuous r=-0.127, p=7e-179), feature_016 (binary, +0.78 months, p=7e-94), feature_028 (-0.81, p=6e-66), feature_018 (+0.32, p=6e-49), feature_025 (r=+0.099, p=3e-109). Many other binaries and continuous features are non-significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable linear model of pfs_months on all features will identify a small set of independently predictive variables and quantify their adjusted effects; weakly-correlated continuous labs (feature_032, feature_030, feature_008, feature_004, feature_013, feature_026, feature_029, feature_007, feature_033, feature_009, feature_010, feature_003, feature_024, feature_019) will drop to non-significance.

analyses.code: OLS(pfs_months ~ all_features, with feature_001 and feature_006 one-hot encoded)
analyses.result_summary: OLS with all features gives R^2=0.894. Independently significant predictors (p<1e-8): feature_015 (+0.168/unit, t=+512), feature_014 (-1.10/level), feature_023 (-1.42), feature_011 (-0.91), feature_016 (+0.89), feature_006_squamous (-0.72), feature_001_former (+0.55) and feature_001_never (+0.55) vs current, feature_017 (-0.073/unit), feature_025 (+0.46/unit), feature_018 (+0.32), feature_031 (-0.20), feature_032 (-0.0004/unit), feature_005 (-0.11). Most other binaries and continuous labs (feature_022, feature_002, feature_028, feature_021, feature_012, feature_020, feature_027, feature_030, feature_008, feature_004, feature_013, feature_026, feature_029, feature_007, feature_033, feature_009, feature_010, feature_003, feature_024, feature_019) drop to p>0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 is a continuous variable on the order of age (range 30-90, mean 65) but its very strong positive association with pfs_months (r=+0.76) is contrary to the typical age-PFS relationship in NSCLC, suggesting it may be a non-age physiologic measure (e.g., physical function score, time-since-diagnosis) or a synthetic predictor; the effect persists after adjustment for histology, smoking, and other strong predictors.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_006')['feature_015'].describe(); same for feature_001, feature_014; quartile means of pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: feature_015 distribution is essentially identical across histology (adeno 65.0 vs squamous 64.9), smoking (current 64.9, former 65.0, never 65.2), and feature_014 levels (65.0, 65.0, 64.9), so it does not confound those associations. Mean pfs_months by feature_015 quartile is monotonic: Q1=1.33, Q2=2.78, Q3=3.97, Q4=5.69 months. Adjusted slope in the full model is +0.168 months per unit (p<1e-300), so feature_015 explains the largest single share of variance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014 is an ordinal performance/stage-like variable: mean pfs_months declines monotonically as feature_014 increases from 0 to 2, with adjusted effect approximately -1.1 months per level.
Tumor histology (feature_006=squamous) is associated with shorter pfs_months than adenocarcinoma after adjusting for smoking, performance, and other covariates.
Current smokers (feature_001=current) have shorter pfs_months than former and never smokers, with the latter two essentially equivalent.

analyses.code: ANOVA pfs_months ~ feature_014; group means; adjusted slope in OLS
analyses.result_summary: Means: feature_014=0 -> 4.33 months (n=17557), =1 -> 3.19 (n=24962), =2 -> 2.15 (n=7481). ANOVA p<1e-300. Adjusted slope in full model = -1.10 months per level (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: OLS coefficient feature_006_squamous in full model
analyses.result_summary: Squamous histology has adjusted PFS effect -0.72 months vs adenocarcinoma (p<1e-300; unadjusted means 2.81 vs 3.67).

analyses.code: ANOVA and dummy coefficients with current as reference
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: current=2.99, former=3.61, never=3.67. Both former (+0.55, p<1e-300) and never (+0.55, p<1e-300) categories are significantly higher than current; former vs never is essentially indistinguishable in adjusted model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_017 is a continuous burden-of-exposure variable (range 0-28, plausibly pack-years equivalent): higher values are associated with shorter pfs_months independently of smoking status.
feature_025 is a continuous variable (range 1.7-5.5, plausibly albumin-like) where higher values are associated with longer pfs_months; the effect persists after adjustment.

analyses.code: Pearson correlation; adjusted OLS slope with feature_001 in model
analyses.result_summary: feature_017 has r=-0.127 with pfs_months (p=7e-179) and adjusted slope -0.073 months/unit (p<1e-300) in the full model that includes smoking, so feature_017 contributes independently of smoking category.

analyses.code: Pearson and OLS slope
analyses.result_summary: feature_025 has unadjusted r=+0.099 (p=3e-109); adjusted slope +0.46 months/unit (p<1e-300) — much larger after controlling for the dominant feature_015 and other prognostic features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_016 is the most plausible 'treatment' variable: it is the only binary indicator with a positive adjusted effect on pfs_months (+0.89 months, prevalence 13%), it is more common in current smokers (20%) than never smokers (2%) and slightly more common in squamous than adenocarcinoma (15% vs 12%) — a plausible pattern for a treatment with non-uniform uptake. By contrast, feature_023 (-1.42, 65% prevalence) and feature_011 (-0.91, 25% prevalence) have large negative effects and are more consistent with stage/biology indicators than treatments.

analyses.code: Cross-tabulation of feature_016 by smoking and histology; comparison of binary main effect signs
analyses.result_summary: feature_016 prevalence by smoking: current 19.7%, former 11.9%, never 2.0%. Among 13 binary features, feature_016 is the only one with a positive adjusted effect on pfs_months. feature_023 (negative, 65%) and feature_011 (negative, 25%) are most plausibly stage/biology markers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity: the effect of feature_016 on pfs_months is modified by other features. A pre-specified screen of feature_016 x covariate interactions in an adjusted OLS model will identify at least one interaction with p<1e-50.

analyses.code: For each candidate covariate: OLS pfs_months ~ baseline_covariates + feature_016 + covariate + feature_016:covariate; record interaction p-value
analyses.result_summary: Top interactions with feature_016 (signed coef, p): feature_018 +2.534 (p<1e-300), feature_031 -1.622 (p<1e-300), feature_028 -0.914 (p=3e-78), feature_005 -0.906 (p=1e-56), feature_015 +0.0079 (p=5e-16). Confirms strong heterogeneity, dominated by feature_018 (positive) and feature_031 (negative).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_016 has essentially no effect on pfs_months in patients with feature_018=0, but a large positive effect (~+2.4 months) in patients with feature_018=1 — i.e., feature_018 is necessary for feature_016 benefit.

analyses.code: Stratify by feature_018, then ttest pfs_months between feature_016=1 and feature_016=0
analyses.result_summary: feature_018=0: feature_016=1 mean 3.213 (n=4122) vs feature_016=0 mean 3.334 (n=28334); diff=-0.121, p=5.4e-4 (no benefit, slightly negative). feature_018=1: feature_016=1 mean 5.766 (n=2246) vs feature_016=0 mean 3.329 (n=15298); diff=+2.437, p=2e-202 (large benefit). Symmetric stratification by feature_016 confirms feature_018 itself has no effect when feature_016=0 (diff=-0.005, p=0.80) but a +2.55 effect when feature_016=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_018=1, feature_016's benefit is further restricted: it is large in feature_031=0 but negligible in feature_031=1 — i.e., feature_031=0 is a second necessary condition for feature_016 benefit.

analyses.code: Stratify by feature_018 x feature_031, then test feature_016 effect within each cell
analyses.result_summary: feature_018=0,feature_031=0: feature_016 effect=-0.10 (p=3e-2). feature_018=0,feature_031=1: -0.14 (p=6e-3). feature_018=1,feature_031=0: +4.56 (p<1e-300). feature_018=1,feature_031=1: -0.17 (p=2e-2). The benefit is essentially confined to the single cell feature_018=1 AND feature_031=0 (n=9776 in subgroup, 1237 with feature_016=1).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the candidate-eligible subgroup feature_018=1 AND feature_031=0, the feature_016 benefit is further attenuated to null when feature_028=1 or feature_005=1 — these binaries act as resistance/exclusion markers that abrogate treatment effect.

analyses.code: Within feature_018=1 & feature_031=0, stratify by feature_028 and by feature_005 and re-estimate feature_016 effect
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_018=1 & feature_031=0: feature_028=0 -> feature_016 effect +4.74 (p<1e-300, n_trt=1191); feature_028=1 -> -0.37 (p=0.29, n_trt=46). feature_005=0 -> +4.71 (p<1e-300, n_trt=1198); feature_005=1 -> +0.08 (p=0.85, n_trt=39). Both feature_028=1 and feature_005=1 abolish the benefit, despite small subgroup sizes.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The full subgroup definition for feature_016 benefit is feature_018=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0; in this subgroup the adjusted feature_016 effect is approximately +4.9 months PFS, while in its complement the adjusted effect is essentially zero.

analyses.code: Define eligible = (feature_018=1 & feature_031=0 & feature_028=0 & feature_005=0); fit pfs_months ~ baseline + feature_016 + interaction(eligible*feature_016) and within-stratum adjusted models
analyses.result_summary: Eligible n=9013 (18% of cohort), of whom 1154 received feature_016. Within eligible: feature_016=1 mean 8.234 vs feature_016=0 mean 3.345; raw diff +4.889, p~0; adjusted (controlling for feature_015, feature_017, feature_025, smoking, histology, feature_014, feature_023, feature_011) +4.922 (p~0). In ineligible (n=40987): adjusted +0.002 (p=0.69). Full model with the eligible*feature_016 interaction has R^2=0.979; main feature_016 effect outside eligible 0.002 (p=0.69), within-eligible additional effect +4.92 (p~0).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 and feature_011 are not treatments but prognostic/stage markers: their effects are uniformly negative across every stratum of every other binary feature (no positive subgroup exists).

analyses.code: For each stratum of each binary, compute feature_023 effect; test whether any stratum has positive sign
analyses.result_summary: feature_023 effect ranges from -1.25 to -1.51 months across all strata of feature_018, feature_031, feature_016, feature_011, feature_022, feature_028, feature_002, feature_005, feature_021, feature_012, feature_020, feature_027 — invariably negative. Largest magnitudes occur in feature_012=0 (-1.49), feature_021=1 (-1.47), feature_018=1 (-1.46). No stratum reverses the sign. The same uniform-negative pattern holds for feature_011. These features behave as stage/biology indicators rather than treatments.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 and feature_031 are pure effect modifiers, not prognostic markers: in the absence of feature_016 (feature_016=0) they have negligible main effects on pfs_months.

analyses.code: Restrict to feature_016=0; ttest pfs_months by feature_018 and by feature_031
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=0: feature_018=1 mean 3.329 vs feature_018=0 mean 3.334 (diff=-0.005, p=0.80). feature_031=1 mean 3.346 vs feature_031=0 mean 3.327 (diff=+0.019, p=0.59). Both null. Within feature_016=1, feature_018 effect is +2.55 and feature_031 effect is -1.68 — substantial. So feature_018 and feature_031 only matter conditional on feature_016 receipt.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Other binary features (feature_022, feature_002, feature_021, feature_012, feature_020, feature_027) are neither treatments nor strong effect modifiers; their adjusted main effects in the full model are < 0.05 months in absolute value and not significant when controlling for the strong predictors.

analyses.code: Inspect adjusted coefficients in full OLS; look for any large interaction with feature_016 (already searched in iteration 7)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted main-effect coefficients (months PFS): feature_022 -0.0006 (p=0.90), feature_021 +0.006 (p=0.10), feature_012 -0.006 (p=0.39), feature_020 +0.002 (p=0.74), feature_027 +0.001 (p=0.88), feature_002 -0.019 (p=0.04). None of these features generated an interaction with feature_016 with |p|<1e-3 in the screen. They appear to be ancillary noise or weak indicators.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_016 treatment benefit, when present, is approximately uniform across performance status (feature_014=0/1/2), histology (adenocarcinoma vs squamous), and smoking categories — i.e., once eligibility (feature_018=1 & feature_031=0) is met, classical clinical modifiers do not further shape the effect size.

analyses.code: Within feature_018=1 & feature_031=0, stratify by feature_014, feature_006, feature_001 and re-estimate feature_016 effect
analyses.result_summary: Within eligible subgroup: feature_014=0 effect +4.55 (p<1e-135), =1 +4.64 (p<1e-204), =2 +4.42 (p<1e-65). Histology adeno +4.60 (p<1e-248), squamous +4.68 (p<1e-147). Smoking former +4.80, current +4.68, never +3.79 (the never group is small, n=23 treated). Effect is broadly homogeneous across these clinical strata at +4.4 to +4.8 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_032 (continuous, range 50-828) has a small but significant negative adjusted effect on pfs_months (~-0.0004 per unit), consistent with a high-cell-count or inflammation-marker pattern (e.g., platelet count, CRP-like) where higher values are associated with worse prognosis.

analyses.code: OLS coefficient feature_032 in full multivariable model
analyses.result_summary: feature_032 adjusted slope -0.00046 months per unit (t=-25.6, p=1.5e-28). Across the inter-quartile range of feature_032 (~150-280) the implied PFS contribution is ~-0.06 months, modest. It does not interact strongly with feature_016 (interaction p=4e-2 in screen).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 has only a weak interaction with feature_016 (the candidate treatment); the absolute treatment benefit in eligible patients is approximately constant in absolute months across the feature_015 distribution rather than scaling with baseline prognosis.

analyses.code: Interaction screen feature_016 x feature_015; within eligible, regress pfs_months ~ feature_016 + feature_015 + feature_016:feature_015
analyses.result_summary: feature_016 x feature_015 interaction coefficient +0.008 (p=5e-16) — directionally positive but tiny relative to the +4.9 month main treatment effect within eligible. Across the feature_015 quartile range (Q1 to Q4), the additional feature_016 benefit varies by less than 0.5 months. Treatment effect on the additive scale is approximately constant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022 is too rare and underpowered to be the locus of a fourth refinement of the eligible subgroup, but its small interaction with feature_016 (-0.054, p=0.17 in screen) implies no clinically meaningful additional restriction to the subgroup definition.

analyses.code: Within eligible subgroup, stratify feature_016 effect by feature_022
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_018=1 & feature_031=0: feature_022=0 (n_trt=1162) feature_016 effect +4.60 (p~0); feature_022=1 (n_trt=75) effect +4.37 (p=4e-23). Both strata show large benefit; feature_022 does not refine eligibility.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Beyond feature_016, no other binary feature in the dataset acts as a treatment with a substantial positive effect concentrated in any biomarker-defined subgroup; all other binaries either have null effects everywhere, uniformly negative effects (stage/biology markers), or behave only as effect modifiers conditional on feature_016.

analyses.code: For each binary feature, scan all 1- and 2-way subgroups defined by other binaries; record the maximum positive subgroup-specific effect of >|1.0| month
analyses.result_summary: After feature_016/feature_018, the next-largest positive subgroup-specific binary effects fall to <|0.3| months and are non-significant or driven by tiny subgroups. No alternative treatment candidate emerges.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A formal interaction OLS confirms that feature_016 has no detectable main effect outside the candidate-eligible subgroup but a +4.9 month effect within it, with the subgroup-by-treatment interaction being highly significant and the residual unexplained variance being small.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ baseline + feature_016 + eligible + feature_016:eligible (with eligible defined per h14)
analyses.result_summary: Full model R^2 = 0.979. feature_016 outside eligible: +0.002 (p=0.69). feature_016 within-eligible additional effect: +4.92 (p<1e-300). Eligible main effect ~0 in absence of treatment (consistent with h16). Strongest covariates: feature_015 (+0.168), feature_014 (-1.11), feature_023 (-1.42), feature_011 (-0.91), feature_006_squamous (-0.73), feature_001_former (+0.55), feature_001_never (+0.55), feature_017 (-0.072), feature_025 (+0.47).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The combination of two ostensibly independent biomarkers (feature_018=1 and feature_031=0) selects an even smaller eligible subset than either alone (~20% of the cohort), demonstrating multiplicative enrichment of treatment-responder phenotype.

analyses.code: Compute joint prevalence of feature_018=1 and feature_031=0 and check independence
analyses.result_summary: Marginal prevalence: feature_018=1 = 35.1%; feature_031=0 = 55.3%. Pairwise correlation -0.007 (essentially independent). Joint feature_018=1 & feature_031=0 prevalence = 19.6%; observed 9776 vs expected under independence 50000 * 0.351 * 0.553 = 9701 — confirming independence. Within this 19.6% subgroup, feature_016 yields +4.6 months PFS vs +0.0 elsewhere.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The implied clinical interpretation is consistent with a biomarker-restricted targeted/immunotherapy regimen (feature_016) whose efficacy requires (a) a positive predictive biomarker (feature_018=1), (b) absence of a primary-resistance biomarker (feature_031=0), and (c) absence of two additional small-prevalence resistance markers (feature_028=0, feature_005=0).

analyses.code: Aggregate: prevalence of each restriction; confidence interval on within-eligible feature_016 effect
analyses.result_summary: Eligible subgroup feature_018=1 & feature_031=0 & feature_028=0 & feature_005=0 has 9013 patients (18.0% of cohort). Adjusted feature_016 effect within eligible: +4.92 months (95% CI +4.90 to +4.95, p<1e-300). Adjusted effect outside eligible: ~0 (95% CI -0.008 to +0.012). The pattern is consistent with a precision-oncology regimen with strict biomarker eligibility.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The smaller +0.32-month adjusted main effect of feature_018 in the unrestricted multivariable model (iteration 2) is entirely attributable to the ~13% of patients in whom feature_018=1 also receive feature_016, not to any prognostic value of feature_018 itself.

analyses.code: Re-run main-effects OLS with addition of feature_016*feature_018 interaction term
analyses.result_summary: When the feature_016 x feature_018 interaction is included, the main effect of feature_018 collapses toward 0 (p>0.5) while the interaction captures the +2.5 effect specific to dual-positive patients. Confirms feature_018 has no independent prognostic value.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Refit best-supported subgroup hypothesis: in NSCLC patients with feature_018=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0, receipt of feature_016 increases pfs_months by approximately +4.9 months relative to non-receipt (positive direction). In all other patients, feature_016 has no effect (essentially 0). Treatment-effect heterogeneity across this dichotomy is highly statistically significant.

analyses.code: Final adjusted OLS confirming subgroup-specific effects; interaction p-value via likelihood-ratio-equivalent F-test on eligible*feature_016 term
analyses.result_summary: Within eligible (n=9013, n_trt=1154): adjusted feature_016 effect = +4.922 months (p<1e-300, t≈+447). Outside eligible (n=40987): adjusted feature_016 effect = +0.002 months (p=0.69). Interaction (eligible*feature_016) is the dominant unexplained source of variance in the otherwise-saturated model. Final treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis: feature_018=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0; treatment = feature_016; outcome = pfs_months; signed direction = +.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Summary: pfs_months in this NSCLC cohort is governed by (a) a dominant continuous prognostic factor feature_015 (~+0.17 mo/unit, ~ explains the bulk of variance), (b) a stable set of additive prognostic features (feature_014, feature_023, feature_011, feature_006, feature_017, feature_025, feature_001), and (c) a single biomarker-restricted treatment effect of feature_016 of approximately +4.9 months in the well-defined eligible subgroup, with no evidence of any second treatment effect in the dataset.

analyses.code: Final saturated model with eligible*feature_016 interaction; report R^2 and component contributions
analyses.result_summary: Final model R^2 = 0.979. Components (signed adjusted contribution): feature_015 +0.168/unit, feature_014 -1.11/level, feature_023 -1.42, feature_011 -0.91, feature_006_squamous -0.73, feature_001_former +0.55, feature_001_never +0.55, feature_017 -0.072/unit, feature_025 +0.47/unit, feature_018 ~0 (effect modifier only), feature_031 ~0 (effect modifier only), feature_016 +0.002 outside eligible, feature_016*eligible +4.92 within eligible. No additional structure detected.
